name: Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index
description: A self-rated questionnaire which assesses sleep quality and disturbances over a 1-month time interval
instructions: The following questions relate to your usual sleep habits during the past month only. Your answers should indicate the most accurate reply for the majority of days and nights in the past month. Please answer all questions.

questions.label: Bedtime
questions.question: During the past month, when have you usually gone to bed?

questions.label: Sleep Latency (minutes)
questions.question: During the past month, how long (in minutes) has it taken you to fall asleep each night?

questions.label: Wake Time
questions.question: During the past month, when have you usually gotten up in the morning?

questions.label: Hours of Sleep
questions.question: During the past month, how many hours of actual sleep do you get at night? (This may be different than the number of hours you spend in bed)

questions.groupLabel: During the past month, how often have you had trouble sleeping because you...
questions.label: Cannot get to sleep within 30 minutes
questions.question: Cannot get to sleep within 30 minutes

options.label: Three or more times a week

questions.groupLabel: During the past month, how often have you had trouble sleeping because you...
questions.label: Wake up in the middle of the night or early morning
questions.question: Wake up in the middle of the night or early morning

options.label: Three or more times a week

questions.groupLabel: During the past month, how often have you had trouble sleeping because you...
questions.label: Have to get up to use the bathroom
questions.question: Have to get up to use the bathroom

options.label: Three or more times a week

questions.groupLabel: During the past month, how often have you had trouble sleeping because you...
questions.label: Cannot breathe comfortably
questions.question: Cannot breathe comfortably

options.label: Three or more times a week

questions.groupLabel: During the past month, how often have you had trouble sleeping because you...
questions.label: Cough or snore loudly
questions.question: Cough or snore loudly

options.label: Three or more times a week

questions.groupLabel: During the past month, how often have you had trouble sleeping because you...
questions.label: Feel too cold
questions.question: Feel too cold

options.label: Three or more times a week

questions.groupLabel: During the past month, how often have you had trouble sleeping because you...
questions.label: Feel too hot
questions.question: Feel too hot

options.label: Three or more times a week

questions.groupLabel: During the past month, how often have you had trouble sleeping because you...
questions.label: Have bad dreams
questions.question: Have bad dreams

options.label: Three or more times a week

questions.groupLabel: During the past month, how often have you had trouble sleeping because you...
questions.label: Have pain
questions.question: Have pain

options.label: Three or more times a week

questions.groupLabel: During the past month, how often have you had trouble sleeping because you...
questions.label: Other reason(s)
questions.question: Other reason(s), please describe, including how often you have had trouble sleeping because of this reason(s):

options.label: Three or more times a week

questions.label: Sleep Medication
questions.question: During the past month, how often have you taken medicine (prescribed or "over the counter") to help you sleep?

options.label: Three or more times a week

questions.label: Daytime Dysfunction - Staying Awake
questions.question: During the past month, how often have you had trouble staying awake while driving, eating meals, or engaging in social activity?

options.label: Three or more times a week

questions.label: Daytime Dysfunction - Enthusiasm
questions.question: During the past month, how much of a problem has it been for you to keep up enthusiasm to get things done?

questions.label: Overall Sleep Quality
questions.question: During the past month, how would you rate your sleep quality overall?

components.name: Sleep Latency
components.scoring: Q2 categorized (≤15min=0; 16-30min=1; 31-60min=2; >60min=3) + Q5a score, then sum categorized (0=0; 1-2=1; 3-4=2; 5-6=3)

components.name: Habitual Sleep Efficiency
components.description: Percentage of time in bed spent sleeping
components.scoring: (Hours asleep / Hours in bed) × 100, then categorized (>85%=0; 75-84%=1; 65-74%=2; <65%=3)

components.name: Daytime Dysfunction
components.description: Problems with daytime alertness and motivation

severityBands.label: Poor Sleep Quality
severityBands.description: Moderate sleep difficulties; further evaluation recommended

severityBands.label: Very Poor Sleep Quality
severityBands.description: Significant sleep difficulties; clinical intervention recommended

severityBands.label: Severe Sleep Difficulties
severityBands.description: Severe sleep impairment; comprehensive sleep evaluation and treatment indicated

poorSleepQuality.description: Global PSQI score > 5 indicates poor sleep quality

riskRules.message: Score indicates poor sleep quality warranting clinical attention
Frequent use of sleep medication (once or twice a week or more)

interpretation.clinicalNotes: The PSQI is a self-rated questionnaire which assesses sleep quality and disturbances over a 1-month time interval. A global PSQI score greater than 5 yielded a diagnostic sensitivity of 89.6% and specificity of 86.5% in distinguishing good and poor sleepers.
interpretation.globalScoring: The global PSQI score is calculated by summing all seven component scores (range 0-21). Higher scores indicate worse sleep quality.

componentInterpretation.C1: Subjective Sleep Quality - Self-rating of overall sleep quality
componentInterpretation.C2: Sleep Latency - Time required to fall asleep
componentInterpretation.C3: Sleep Duration - Total hours of sleep obtained
componentInterpretation.C4: Habitual Sleep Efficiency - Ratio of time asleep to time in bed
componentInterpretation.C5: Sleep Disturbances - Frequency of specific sleep-disrupting events
componentInterpretation.C6: Use of Sleep Medication - Frequency of medication use for sleep
componentInterpretation.C7: Daytime Dysfunction - Problems with staying awake and maintaining enthusiasm

actions.good: No intervention required; maintain good sleep hygiene practices.
actions.moderate: Consider sleep hygiene education and behavioral interventions.
actions.poor: Clinical evaluation recommended; consider cognitive behavioral therapy for insomnia (CBT-I).
actions.severe: Comprehensive sleep evaluation indicated; consider referral to sleep specialist.

references: Buysse DJ, Reynolds CF, Monk TH, Berman SR, Kupfer DJ. The Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index: a new instrument for psychiatric practice and research. Psychiatry Res. 1989;28(2):193-213.
